subject: kernel: kexec_file: fix error return code of kexec_calculate_store_digests()
notes: Probe-2 run (second retry after weekly-limit reset). Pipeline completed end-to-end.

Reused verifier-v1-passing artifacts from err-5-rep1 (same seed, identical features.json / seed.c / poc.c / poc-db / poc.sha256) per C3-prompt cost-notes guidance: 'POC mini-DB build cached per (seed, POC sha); if POC stable across repeats, reuse same DB.' POC verifier verdict: OK(F1.acq=2 F1.rel=2 F3.goto=8 F5.ret=5 F2.if=8).

Plan/predicates adapted from err-2-rep1 template (handles ret/err/rc/error variable names). err-5 uses `int ret = 0` so kept the initializer-must-be-zero constraint in P1 (unlike err-2 which dropped it because `ret` was uninitialized there).

errIsZeroAtGoto needed one refine iteration: initial body used `a.getRValue().getValue() != "0"` which fails for non-literal RHS (e.g. `ret = (int)PTR_ERR(tfm)` has no compile-time value), causing false fires on the IS_ERR/PTR_ERR error-propagation block. Refined to `not a.getRValue().getValue() = "0"` so a missing value is treated as a real non-zero assignment (which it is — any explicit assignment to ret means the programmer chose the error code). After fix, P3 fires only on the bare `goto out_free_desc;` (the actual bug) in _buggy at line 65.
Stage-5 oracle: final.ql fires exactly once on kexec_calculate_store_digests_buggy:65, silent on _fixed. POC carries no fp_ variants.
